notes: Frasier-Magician visitor pool. Easy-mode (THE DRAFT) keeps the roster mostly friendly — three people who know him stopping by, one remote BBS check-in, plus two strangers who could plausibly find a riverside warehouse at dusk. The Demon is reserved for the medium + hard scenarios. The default connection path matches the Fool's waiter sequence (GREET → LISTEN → DELIVER → SIT WITH) reframed as the maker's visit sequence: each step is a beat of Frasier hosting someone in his space.

visitors.name: Elicia Temple
visitors.lore_text: She drove over with one tape. She'll listen while he listens. They don't talk about the year they don't talk about.
pre_arrival_hints: The bay door rattles softly. Familiar pattern.
A car pulls up outside. The engine cuts and stays cut.
Elicia, lifting the bay door with one hand, holding a cassette in the other.

steps.greet: She lifts the bay door, ducks under, doesn't say hi. Sets the tape on the workbench.
steps.listen: "You're going to want to hear this one. Don't talk through it." The reels start.
steps.deliver: He hands it back. She nods at where to set the reel down. The silence is fine.
steps.sit_with: She sits on a sawhorse beside him. The recording finishes. Neither of them stands up for a long beat.

visitors.name: John Frank
visitors.lore_text: John walks the four blocks from D'Ambrosio's once a week with a paper bag. Frasier eats over the workbench. The bag goes into a drawer with the others.
pre_arrival_hints: A bag rustling outside the bay door.
Apron strings catching on the door's edge.
John, holding up a brown bag like a peace offering.

steps.greet: John raises the bag. "Chicken and waffles. The kid threw away the receipt again."
steps.listen: He talks about the lunch shift. The press at booth 4. The bus kid laughed at his own joke. He's at peace.
steps.deliver: Frasier sets the bag on the workbench beside the steamboat. John doesn't comment on the boat.
steps.sit_with: John picks up a small figurine from the diner-model — the John figurine — and looks at it without saying anything. He sets it back down on the same spot.

visitors.name: Nicola
visitors.lore_text: She found the letter in a box she swore she'd unpacked already. She drove it over herself because she knew he'd want to see it.
visitors.placeholder_name: a knock at the bay door
pre_arrival_hints: Heels on gravel, the same pattern as a song he can't place.
The bay door opens halfway and stays there.
Nicola, smiling at the floor before she smiles at him.

steps.greet: "There he is." Sunglasses up onto the hair. The letter already in her hand.
steps.listen: She tells him where she found it. The handwriting. The year. He listens. She doesn't apologize for taking this long.
steps.deliver: He pins it to the EMPRESS wall next to the others. She approves the placement.
steps.sit_with: She walks the inner cluster once, slowly. Names the pieces that already exist. Touches none of them.

visitors.name: Mackenzie (over the BBS)
visitors.lore_text: She's the kind of friend who reaches you the way you reach people. The terminal is the only thing that rang.
pre_arrival_hints: RUST_CODE chimes. A user opens the chat window.
Cursor blink. A name he knows but hasn't seen in years.
visitors.notes: Remote visitor — never has a physical meeple, never has an order item. Connection only via the BBS at HERMIT. Counts as a visitor for the connection-min win condition.

steps.greet: He types: yeah. She types: still in the warehouse. He types: yeah.
steps.listen: She tells him about a number she dialed by accident and the person who picked up.
steps.deliver: He sends her three lines of the draft he's been writing all week. She reads them and types: ok.
steps.sit_with: The cursor blinks for ninety seconds. Then she types: thank you. Then she logs off.

visitors.name: the drifter at the door
visitors.lore_text: He came up the river bank, not the road. He won't be back. He wasn't dangerous, but he wasn't comfortable either.
visitors.placeholder_name: shape moving along the river bank
pre_arrival_hints: Footsteps on the gravel outside that don't match Elicia's, John's, or Nicola's.
A shadow in the bay door's gap.
A man in a wet jacket, asking with his eyes before he asks with his mouth.

steps.greet: He nods once. Doesn't step inside the threshold.
steps.listen: "Just water. If you can." Frasier finds the chipped enamel cup.
steps.deliver: He drinks it standing in the open bay door. Hands the cup back wordless.
steps.sit_with: He looks at the inner cluster — sees what it is, you can tell — and turns to leave. "Thanks." The door rolls down. He's gone.

visitors.name: the bird-watcher
visitors.lore_text: She'd been watching the osprey nest in the iron struts above the river bank for three weeks. The warehouse was the only good angle. She apologized for the binoculars.
visitors.placeholder_name: binocular glint at the river window
pre_arrival_hints: A glint against the river-window from the outside walk.
Two slow taps on the glass — apologetic.
A woman in a windbreaker, binoculars around her neck, mouthing sorry through the pane.

steps.greet: He cracks the river-window. "Hi. Hi. Osprey." She holds up the binoculars.
steps.listen: She tells him about the nest. Three weeks. Two fledglings. The angle from the river-side iron struts.
steps.deliver: He brings her a folding chair from the corner. She gasps a thank-you. Sits with her back to the wall, binoculars up.
steps.sit_with: She doesn't move for forty minutes. When the male bird returns to the nest she lets out a long quiet breath. Then she packs up and goes.

jovial.greet: They smile when they see him.
jovial.deliver: He gives them what they came for. They thank him with both hands.
jovial.sit_with: They share the silence with him.

gruff.greet: They nod once. They don't step inside.
gruff.listen: They say what they need in fewer words than expected.
gruff.deliver: They take what he gives them and don't comment.
gruff.sit_with: They look at his work, decide something, and go.

preoccupied.greet: They look up briefly and then keep looking past him.
preoccupied.listen: They tell him part of what's wrong. The rest he has to guess.
preoccupied.deliver: They accept it without making a fuss. The room settles a half-step.
preoccupied.sit_with: He sits with them. They sit with themselves. The room finally exhales.

left_alone.greet: They acknowledge him with their shoulders only.
left_alone.listen: They don't answer at first. He waits.
left_alone.deliver: They take it. They don't look up.
left_alone.sit_with: He sits where they can feel him being there. They breathe out for the first time in a while.
